channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: US probing Teslas over driverless feature
item.description: The U.S. government is probing Tesla over a driverless feature in its electric vehicles, according to the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA).
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Flying taxis are on the horizon as aviation soars into a new frontier
item.description: When he was still a boy making the long, tedious trips between his school and his woodsy home in the mountains during the 1980s, JoeBen Bevirt began fantasizing about flying cars that could whisk him to his destination in a matter of minutes.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How Spotify is turning podcasting into social media
item.description: When Spotify introduced video podcasts, its interface started to look more like TikTok. Now the music streaming giant has added a glorified creator fund.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia Unveils New Products and Partnerships at CES 2025
item.description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang unveiled a suite of new products, services, and partnerships at CES 2025, driving innovation in gaming, robotics, personal computing, and self-driving vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: AI potential meets ROI pragmatism: 3 crucial questions every CIO should ask
item.description: If you had to name 2023’s single-most impactful and disruptive technology, you’d need just two letters: AI. With the release of OpenAI’s ChatGPT in November 2022, we watched a tsunami of AI news and noise throughout the year. And there’s no sign of things slowing down.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Lenovo Legion Go S: A New Era in PC Gaming Handhelds?
item.description: Lenovo launches a new PC gaming handheld, the Legion Go S, with a SteamOS license and improved specs.
item.dc:creator: John.Loeffler@futurenet.com (John Loeffler), TechRadar

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nvidia Unveils Supercharged Mini PC with Blackwell Architecture - Tech News
item.description: Nvidia reveals Project DIGITS, a mini PC designed to make AI accessible to more users through the company's flagship Blackwell hardware.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Meta scraps fact-checking in favor of X-style Community Notes
item.description: CEO Mark Zuckerberg acknowledged that the changes are in part sparked by political events including Donald Trump’s election.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Meta's new board includes UFC boss Dana White, a familiar figure in Trump's orbit
item.description: Meta has appointed three new members to its board of directors, including Dana White, the president and CEO of Ultimate Fighting Championship and a familiar figure in the orbit of incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Until Dawn's movie adaptation won't follow the same story
item.description: Sony's Until Dawn movie won't be a direct adaptation of Supermassive Games' horror title of the same name.
item.dc:creator: lucy.buglass@futurenet.com (Lucy Buglass), TechRadar

item.title: Santa Clara’s Great America theme park lease could be terminated in three years: new docs
item.description: The lease that enables California's Great America amusement park to operate in Santa Clara might be terminated in about three years.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Opinion: California, don’t let 3 companies hog all the AI infrastructure
item.description: The massive computer power needed to train AI should be a public resource, not controlled by private tech firms.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Foster, The Mercury News

item.title: US ups pressure on Tencent by designating it a Chinese military company
item.description: US adds Tencent and others to an entity list composed of companies believed to be related to the Chinese military.
item.dc:creator: benedict.collins@futurenet.com (Benedict Collins), TechRadar

item.title: Nueve propósitos de TI para 2025
item.description: Los CIO son ambiciosos. No son del tipo que se conforma con el statu quo y se han fijado grandes objetivos para el año entrante, según innumerables encuestas realizadas a ejecutivos de TI.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI redefines customer centricity, beyond language, region for IndiaMART: Nikhil Prabhakar
item.description: In a short span of 28 years, IndiaMART has emerged as India’s chosen B2B marketplace and has increased ease of business while simultaneously creating a connected marketplace for all buyers and suppliers across the country.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: BBVA migra su plataforma de datos a la nube de AWS en el Viejo Continente
item.description: En 2023, BBVA eligió a AWS para acelerar su transformación basada en datos; un año después, se puede decir que la plataforma global de datos en la nube del banco es una realidad.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Python Wins Tiobe Programming Language of the Year Award for 2024
item.description: Python has won the Tiobe programming language of the year award for 2024, given to the language with the highest increase in ratings in Tiobe’s index of language popularity during the year.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: British prime minister condemns 'lies and misinformation' after Musk attacks
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer condemned the “lies and misinformation” undermining the United Kingdom’s democracy and his record after Elon Musk’s continued attacks.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft will invest $80B in AI data centers in fiscal 2025
item.description: Microsoft is on track to invest around $80 billion this fiscal year to build out AI-enabled data centers, company president Brad Smith said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OPM CIO Cavallo retiring after 23 years in government
item.description: Guy Cavallo has been the Office of Personnel Management’s CIO since 2020 and also worked at SBA, TSA and began his career working for the Senate. The post OPM CIO Cavallo retiring after 23 years in government first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Fubo combining with Disney's Hulu + Live TV; lawsuit against Venu Sports settled
item.description: Disney's Hulu + Live TV and sports streaming service Fubo are combining in a deal that will also see the settlement of a lawsuit against the creation of Venu Sports.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hacked Chrome extensions put 2.6 million users at risk of data leak
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says hackers are embedding dangerous code in Chrome extensions to steal your data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: It is easier than ever to disable Location Sharing on your Android phone
item.description: Google Maps Location Sharing is now built into Android settings; no need for Maps settings. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses the welcome improvement for Android users.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cyber security agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to expect from NaaS in 2025
item.description: Enterprises have been slow to adopt NaaS for a number of reasons, including lack of clarity on exactly what NaaS is, lack of a solid business case that NaaS saves money over the long term, and questions around security, visibility, control, and data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: My robot teacher: The challenge of AI in computer science education
item.description: Over the past two years, generative AI has been a force for transformation—and disruption—everywhere it’s landed. Education was no exception; if anything, schools were among the first institutions to grapple with AI’s implications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Ditch boring emoji and create your own unique versions with Genmoji on iPhone
item.description: Genmoji, the latest addition to Apple's Apple Intelligence suite, uses artificial intelligence to generate customized emoji based on text descriptions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Treasury hack: Lawmakers seek more details on scope, third-party vulnerabilities
item.description: The Treasury hack is sparking new questions about how agencies ensure the security of third-party technology service providers. The post Treasury hack: Lawmakers seek more details on scope, third-party vulnerabilities first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took police more than a week to publicly identify Debrina Kawam, 57, as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train last month. But on the internet, it took just hours for a false name to begin spreading.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Apple needs good AI acquisition hires
item.description: When it comes to artificial intelligence development, Apple seems more interested than usual in seeking out good acquisition targets, potentially building on the small acquisitions it makes most months. The company has, in fact, led the industry when it comes to strategic AI acquisitions since 2023.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: No surprise: AI, cyber continued to dominate in 2024
item.description: Current and former federal technology executives weighed in on what topics drove the storylines in 2024. The post No surprise: AI, cyber continued to dominate in 2024 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network
